title: [BUG] Cannot save scorer results when using PostgresStore
body:
### Describe the Bug

I'm playing around with the new Scorers functionality and when a score gets generated the insert fails when using PostgresStore because no ID is being provided:

```
Failing row contains (null, custom, null, defaultTranscriptMedicalRelevanceAgent, {"id": "custom", "name": "Custom Scorer", "description": "A cust..., null, null, 42, null, {"structuredOutput": false}, null, null, null, [{"id": "3125cc22-4830-43e2-9e9f-a7c609f74e1f", "role": "user", ..., {"text": "Contains medical data: False\\n\\nJustification:\\nThe pr..., null, {}, AGENT, {"id": "Transcript Medical Relevance Checker", "name": "Transcri..., Transcript Medical Relevance Checker, LIVE, null, null, 2025-07-25 21:41:14.849, 2025-07-25 21:41:14.849, 2025-07-25 21:41:14.849+00, 2025-07-25 21:41:14.849+00).
```

When using LibSQLStore it works as expected.

I'd be happy to contribute a fix for this if I could get some guidance and desired outcome here. Should we fix this at the schema level by setting a default value in the DB? 

Alternatively I can see that the `uuid` package is already available so I could just pass in a generated ID as part of the insert. I'll go with this approach for right now to get a PR up.

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Update Mastra instance to use PostgresStore for storage
2. Add a scorer to an agent
3. Interact with the agent
4. Observe that the insert fails with the above error

### Expected Behavior

The score should be saved properly.

### Environment Information

_No response_

### Verification

- [x] I have searched the existing issues to make sure this is not a duplicate
- [x] I have included sufficient information for the team to reproduce and understand the issue

linked_prs.title: fix: ensure score id is generated for postgres store
